items.limitationEn: The study focuses on student learning outcomes, not the long-term transfer of clinical skills or patient outcomes.

items.title: Intravaginal dehydroepiandrosterone for the treatment of vulvovaginal atrophy: a systematic review and meta-analysis
items.summary: A systematic review and meta-analysis of studies up to July 2025 confirmed the therapeutic efficacy of intravaginal dehydroepiandrosterone (DHEA) for vulvovaginal atrophy in postmenopausal women, showing improvements in vaginal dryness, dyspareunia, and quality of life.
items.curatedReason: 这项系统综述证实了阴道内脱氢表雄酮（DHEA）对绝经后妇女外阴阴道萎缩（VVA）的治疗效果。若你的患者群体中有绝经后女性，可将其作为评估VVA症状的潜在治疗选项，并转诊至相关专科医师进行药物管理。
items.titleZh: 阴道内脱氢表雄酮治疗外阴阴道萎缩：一项系统综述和Meta分析
items.summaryZh: 一项截至2025年7月的系统综述和Meta分析证实了阴道内脱氢表雄酮（DHEA）对绝经后妇女外阴阴道萎缩的治疗效果，显示阴道干燥、性交困难和生活质量均有改善。
items.curatedReasonEn: This systematic review confirms the therapeutic efficacy of intravaginal dehydroepiandrosterone (DHEA) for vulvovaginal atrophy (VVA) in postmenopausal women. If your patient population includes postmenopausal women, consider this as a potential treatment option for VVA symptoms, and refer to relevant specialists for medication management.
items.limitation: 研究未详细说明药物治疗与物理治疗结合的效果。
items.limitationEn: The study does not detail the effects of combining pharmaceutical treatment with physical therapy.

items.title: ChainBMD: A deep learning framework for stroke evaluation and guidance using kinetic chain graphs and temporal analysis - ScienceDirect
items.summary: Researchers developed ChainBMD, a deep learning framework for stroke evaluation and guidance using kinetic chain graphs and temporal analysis. It aims to improve human motion evaluation and provide training guidance through interpretable wearable sensing.
items.curatedReason: 这项研究提出了一个基于深度学习的卒中评估和指导框架ChainBMD，利用运动链图和时间分析。如果你对智能康复技术和客观评估方法感兴趣，这为卒中患者的运动评估和训练提供了新的视角。
items.titleZh: ChainBMD：一个利用运动链图和时间分析进行卒中评估与指导的深度学习框架
items.summaryZh: 研究人员开发了ChainBMD，一个利用运动链图和时间分析进行卒中评估和指导的深度学习框架。它旨在通过可解释的穿戴式传感来改进人体运动评估并提供训练指导。
items.curatedReasonEn: This study introduces ChainBMD, a deep learning framework for stroke evaluation and guidance using kinetic chain graphs and temporal analysis. If you are interested in intelligent rehabilitation technologies and objective assessment methods, this offers a new perspective on motor evaluation and training for stroke patients.
items.limitation: 作为深度学习框架的初步研究，其在真实临床环境中的泛化性和长期有效性尚待验证。
items.limitationEn: As a preliminary study of a deep learning framework, its generalizability and long-term effectiveness in real clinical settings still need to be validated.

items.title: Branched-chain amino acids alleviate Parkinson’s neurodegeneration via microbial propionate-dependent restoration of the PI3K/Akt/mTORC1 axis - ScienceDirect
items.summary: This research explored how branched-chain amino acids (BCAAs) alleviate Parkinson’s neurodegeneration via microbial propionate-dependent restoration of the PI3K/Akt/mTORC1 axis, highlighting the gut–brain axis in Parkinson's disease pathogenesis.
items.curatedReason: 这项研究探讨了支链氨基酸（BCAAs）通过肠道微生物丙酸盐依赖的PI3K/Akt/mTORC1通路来缓解帕金森病神经退行性病变。作为物理治疗师，理解帕金森病的多系统影响（包括肠脑轴），有助于你更全面地考虑患者的整体健康状况，并与其他医疗专业人员协作。
items.titleZh: 支链氨基酸通过微生物丙酸盐依赖的PI3K/Akt/mTORC1轴恢复来缓解帕金森病神经退行性变
items.summaryZh: 这项研究探讨了支链氨基酸（BCAAs）如何通过微生物丙酸盐依赖的PI3K/Akt/mTORC1轴恢复来缓解帕金森病神经退行性变，强调了肠脑轴在帕金森病发病机制中的作用。
items.curatedReasonEn: This study investigates how branched-chain amino acids (BCAAs) alleviate Parkinson's neurodegeneration via microbial propionate-dependent PI3K/Akt/mTORC1 axis. As a physical therapist, understanding the multi-system impact of Parkinson's disease (including the gut-brain axis) helps you consider patients' overall health more comprehensively and collaborate better with other healthcare professionals.
items.limitation: 这是一项机制研究，主要针对帕金森病神经退行性病变的分子通路，尚未直接涉及临床康复干预。
items.limitationEn: This is a mechanistic study, primarily focusing on molecular pathways of Parkinson's neurodegeneration, and does not directly involve clinical rehabilitation interventions.

items.title: [Relationship between the consistency of auxiliary provocation tests with the supine roll test and treatment outcomes for horizontal semicircular canal benign paroxysmal positional vertigo]
items.summary: This study investigated the relationship between the consistency of auxiliary provocation tests with the supine roll test and treatment outcomes for horizontal semicircular canal benign paroxysmal positional vertigo (HSC-BPPV).
items.curatedReason: 这项研究探讨了辅助性激发试验与仰卧滚转试验在水平半规管良性阵发性位置性眩晕（HSC-BPPV）中的一致性及其与治疗结局的关系。如果你在诊断和治疗眩晕症，这项研究提供了关于HSC-BPPV诊断准确性和预后预测的见解，指导你的手法操作选择。
items.titleZh: 辅助性激发试验与仰卧滚转试验在水平半规管良性阵发性位置性眩晕中的一致性及其与治疗结局的关系
items.summaryZh: 这项研究调查了辅助性激发试验与仰卧滚转试验在水平半规管良性阵发性位置性眩晕（HSC-BPPV）中的一致性及其与治疗结局的关系。
items.curatedReasonEn: This study explores the consistency between auxiliary provocation tests and the supine roll test in horizontal semicircular canal benign paroxysmal positional vertigo (HSC-BPPV) and its relationship with treatment outcomes. If you diagnose and treat vertigo, this study offers insights into diagnostic accuracy and prognostic prediction for HSC-BPPV, guiding your manual technique choices.
items.limitation: 研究未指明具体研究设计，可能存在观察性研究的局限性。
items.limitationEn: The specific study design is not specified, potentially carrying limitations inherent to observational studies.

items.title: Risk Factors for Musculoskeletal Injuries in Military Recruits: The Impact of Sex, Body Fat Percentage, and Physical Fitness
items.summary: A prospective interventional study investigated the incidence of musculoskeletal symptoms and injuries in male and female recruits during sixteen weeks of Brazilian Marine Basic Military Training, and assessed the impact of sex, body fat percentage, and physical fitness as risk factors.
items.curatedReason: 这项研究探讨了军事新兵肌肉骨骼损伤的风险因素，特别是性别、体脂百分比和体能的影响。如果你在运动人群中工作，特别是涉及高强度训练或特定性别差异的群体，这能帮助你识别高风险个体，并制定更具针对性的预防策略。
items.titleZh: 军事新兵肌肉骨骼损伤的风险因素：性别、体脂百分比和体能的影响
items.summaryZh: 一项前瞻性干预研究调查了巴西海军基础军事训练16周期间男女新兵肌肉骨骼症状和损伤的发生率，并评估了性别、体脂百分比和体能作为风险因素的影响。
items.curatedReasonEn: This study investigates risk factors for musculoskeletal injuries in military recruits, particularly the impact of sex, body fat percentage, and physical fitness. If you work with athletic populations, especially those involved in high-intensity training or with specific sex differences, this can help you identify high-risk individuals and develop more targeted prevention strategies.
items.limitation: 研究对象是军事新兵，其损伤模式和风险因素可能不完全适用于一般运动人群。
items.limitationEn: The study subjects are military recruits, whose injury patterns and risk factors may not be fully generalizable to the general athletic population.

items.title: Single leg vertical jump performance identifies knee function deficits ...
items.summary: This original research highlights that single-leg vertical jump performance can identify knee function deficits at return to sport after ACL reconstruction in male athletes, providing a critical objective measure for readiness.
items.curatedReason: 这项研究强调了单腿垂直跳表现能识别男性运动员ACL重建后重返运动时的膝关节功能缺陷。如果你在评估ACL术后患者的重返运动准备情况，应将单腿垂直跳作为关键评估指标之一，以更客观地判断膝关节功能对称性。
items.titleZh: 单腿垂直跳表现可识别男性运动员ACL重建后重返运动时的膝关节功能缺陷
items.summaryZh: 这项原创研究强调，单腿垂直跳表现可以识别男性运动员ACL重建后重返运动时的膝关节功能缺陷，为判断重返运动准备情况提供了一个关键的客观测量指标。
items.curatedReasonEn: This study emphasizes that single-leg vertical jump performance can identify knee function deficits in male athletes returning to sport after ACL reconstruction. If you are assessing readiness for return-to-sport in post-ACL patients, include single-leg vertical jump as a key assessment metric to objectively evaluate knee functional symmetry.
items.limitation: 研究对象仅限于男性运动员，结果不一定适用于女性或其他人群。
items.limitationEn: The study subjects were limited to male athletes, and the results may not be generalizable to females or other populations.

items.title: Progressing rehabilitation after injury: consider the 'control-chaos ...
items.summary: This article proposes considering the 'control-chaos continuum' when progressing rehabilitation after injury, advocating for a nuanced approach to re-integrating athletes into training and match play while balancing reinjury risk and performance optimization.
items.curatedReason: 这篇文章提出了“控制-混沌连续体”的概念，用于指导损伤后康复进展和重返运动。如果你在制定运动员康复方案，可以采纳这个框架，渐进式地引入混沌元素，以更好地模拟运动环境，降低再损伤风险。
items.titleZh: 损伤后康复进展：考虑“控制-混沌连续体”
items.summaryZh: 这篇文章提出在损伤后康复进展中考虑“控制-混沌连续体”，主张在将运动员重新整合到训练和比赛中时，采用一种细致入微的方法，平衡再损伤风险与表现优化。
items.curatedReasonEn: This article introduces the 'control-chaos continuum' concept for guiding rehabilitation progression and return-to-sport after injury. If you are developing rehabilitation protocols for athletes, adopt this framework to progressively introduce chaotic elements, better simulating sports environments and reducing re-injury risk.
items.limitation: 这是一篇观点文章，而非基于新的研究数据，其指导意义需结合实践经验进行判断。
items.limitationEn: This is a perspective article, not based on new research data; its guidance should be evaluated in conjunction with practical experience.

items.title: Advances in research on the role of VEGF family members in ischemic stroke - ScienceDirect
items.summary: This review article summarizes advances in research on the role of VEGF family members in ischemic stroke, highlighting their dual roles in ischemic stroke and cerebral ischemia/reperfusion injury, and their potential in promoting angiogenesis and neuroprotection.
items.curatedReason: 这篇综述探讨了VEGF家族成员在缺血性卒中中的作用进展，提示了神经保护和血管生成的潜在机制。作为物理治疗师，了解这些基础科学进展，能帮助你理解卒中后生理恢复的潜在靶点，并与医疗团队更好地协作。
items.titleZh: VEGF家族成员在缺血性卒中中作用研究进展
items.summaryZh: 这篇综述文章总结了VEGF家族成员在缺血性卒中中作用的研究进展，强调了其在缺血性卒中和脑缺血/再灌注损伤中的双重作用，以及在促进血管生成和神经保护方面的潜力。
items.curatedReasonEn: This review discusses advancements in understanding the role of VEGF family members in ischemic stroke, suggesting potential mechanisms for neuroprotection and angiogenesis. As a physical therapist, understanding these basic science advancements can help you grasp potential targets for physiological recovery post-stroke and collaborate more effectively with the medical team.
items.limitation: 这是一篇综述文章，主要基于现有研究的总结，不提供新的临床数据。
items.limitationEn: This is a review article, primarily summarizing existing research, and does not provide new clinical data.
